## [00:00 - 03:00]

What up guys, cool here. What we're going to talk about right now is the email marketing strategy we use. And really, we did already cover the strategy, OK? Because the content strategy is basically the same as we use for Facebook groups, OK? So we usually just do one strategy, and then those same pieces of content, a lot of them either just get repurposed for email, or we use email to send people from email to our Facebook group for like a Facebook Live, Facebook Training, something like that. But now for email, there's a few distinctions. So first of all, we're always going to use these first three emails here that I'm going to show you to incentivize open and click ASAP, OK? So we send the first email. It's just, hey, video download, promise. And then I just essentially say, hey, thanks for downloading this. You can download it here, PS. And then I link the free bonus, which is our free sales course. And then I say, click here to go to the free sales course. You'll have to get into my Facebook group. But when you get in, it's 30 plus hours of content, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah. So obviously, this email, because it's the first one, they're looking for it. They're actually opted in for this. It's going to be the highest open rate email. All right? It's also going to be the highest click-through email. So you want to drive into the Facebook group here. And then in two hours, we send another email for our free bonus, which is the course again. So we send again for the course and say, read the email to get the course. Here's why you should get the course. Here's what to do next to get the course. Then we link it here. Make sure you don't do reply to. We actually did change this to a link. And the first couple of emails we found the link was a little bit better than reply to. And by the way, for our daily campaigns thereafter, so our campaign blast, we do do reply to. So nothing wrong with that. Just for this particular email, we had better throughput on the click-through. So we do this free course next. That's two hours later. It's the free bonus. I like free course, but you can do any fat, juicy bonus. And then again, we're driving to the group here. And we're also incentivizing the open because we're giving away a great thing here. And then we'll do one more after this. This is a last reminder. So it's like last reminder to get your course, last chance to get your course. We do one more. After those three emails, if they don't open any of those three, we'll never email them again until they open any one of those three. So they could open a year later, and then they'll get segmented back into a list that we call opens. And then we'll start sending them campaigns like a daily email. But we only send to opens. So they have to open these three to ever get sent again. So I'm not like the world's greatest email expert here. But when we started doing this, our results went way up. Our deliverability went way up. Open rates, obviously, are going to go way up if you're sending to opens. And our ISPs on our server, I just think are so much better. And so ISPs are what's going to really dictate your email deliverability. So anyways, that is what we do first for those first couple of emails. Now what we also do is we use text-based emails for as many as we can. So like an active campaign, essentially, there's an option that says start from scratch. And then when you say start from scratch, there's HTML and there's text.

## [03:00 - 06:03]

Now, most people just never use text. But I have found text, even though the downside is you can't track opens, I've personally found text you're able to get way better deliverability and also just overall better response. I think just because it looks like a personal email. And why would you not want to look native to the platform you're advertising on? This makes sense to me. Now, we do do HTML sometimes. For instance, if I have a really cool case study screenshot, I will do an HTML email for that. But most of the times we do text. And when I do do HTML, I try to make it look as much like text as possible. But usually HTML will pull better than text. We also already said this, but we only send it opens after the first three emails. We're going to send usually three to four times weekly, sometimes five, six times weekly. I think daily or five times a week is a really, really good number. All of our call of actions right now are reply sales reps if you want us to send us details on how recruiting services work, for instance. So we do reply a term if you want information. Or we'll do that's more of an intent-based reply. We'll also do reply sales if you want my sales training on how I closed 40% for three years, blah, blah, blah, blah. So we can cut a two-step it for value. And we can also do an intent-based one for the offer. Obviously, your two steps for value are going to be handled differently. You're going to have higher throughput, but they're going to be handled differently than what you're doing in terms of an intent-based reply. So that's what I was saying here. Info, so a direct script, or a specific training, like a two-step, that's the indirect script. And we'll cover this later in the training. But this is more direct script. There's a direct messenger script that totally transposes fine to email. This is an indirect script that we use for messenger that this will also work on email. And then basically what I do is I, and you could do round robin here, I like, because my setters already have so much opportunity, I just give the one who gets the most close sets from the last week my email inbox for the following week. Now I might change that in the future. That's how I do it right now. So they're in there all day every day, just replying back, using the direct or indirect script respectively, doing as soon as they reply back. So as soon as we get the reply coming in an email, the setter is obviously going to reply using the script. That's order number one. But also what they'll do is they'll take the email, they'll punch it into our CRM, and then they'll load up the PowerDialer, then they'll double call them off of a local dialer. We use Allaware integrated with Close. Then they'll send them a text, and they'll send them a voicemail. Then third, they're going to keep following up using all of these channels until the triage call is booked. And they're going to have a triage call, and then lead that to a consult. So the triage call, the purpose of that is to sell the sales call. That's not the sales call. It's to sell the sales call. If you're doing a two-call close process, maybe you're doing a qualifying call, a demo. And it's more like very, very B2B type of stuff, agency services. Then really, you're going to be selling the demo in a lot of cases. Now, one thing we've also done, just so you guys can see,

is we've also done, if interested, Texas number. And obviously, it's not like a letter here.
